verses.text: Israel sets out and stops at Beersheba — the place his father Isaac met God. He offers sacrifices to the God of his father. That night God calls him in a vision: Jacob, Jacob. Here am I.
Israel sets out and stops at Beersheba — the place his father Isaac met God. He offers sacrifices to the God of his father. That night God calls him in a vision: Jacob, Jacob. Here am I.

Do not fear to go down into Egypt, God says. I will make of thee a great nation there. I will go down with thee, and I will surely bring thee up again. And Joseph shall close thine eyes. The migration is blessed before it begins.

Do not fear to go down into Egypt, God says. I will make of thee a great nation there. I will go down with thee, and I will surely bring thee up again. And Joseph shall close thine eyes. The migration is blessed before it begins.

Jacob and his sons rise up; they put him and the little ones and the wives in the wagons Pharaoh sent. They take their livestock and possessions and journey to Egypt — Jacob and every descendant with him. His sons, his sons' sons, his daughters, all together.

Jacob and his sons rise up; they put him and the little ones and the wives in the wagons Pharaoh sent. They take their livestock and possessions and journey to Egypt — Jacob and every descendant with him. His sons, his sons' sons, his daughters, all together.

Jacob and his sons rise up; they put him and the little ones and the wives in the wagons Pharaoh sent. They take their livestock and possessions and journey to Egypt — Jacob and every descendant with him. His sons, his sons' sons, his daughters, all together.

These are the names of the children of Israel who came into Egypt. Reuben's sons: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, Carmi. Simeon's sons. Levi's sons: Gershon, Kohath, Merari — the line that will later be set apart for the priesthood.

These are the names of the children of Israel who came into Egypt. Reuben's sons: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, Carmi. Simeon's sons. Levi's sons: Gershon, Kohath, Merari — the line that will later be set apart for the priesthood.

These are the names of the children of Israel who came into Egypt. Reuben's sons: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, Carmi. Simeon's sons. Levi's sons: Gershon, Kohath, Merari — the line that will later be set apart for the priesthood.
Judah's sons: Er and Onan died in Canaan; Pharez and Zarah, with Hezron and Hamul. Issachar's and Zebulun's lines. Leah bore these and her daughter Dinah — thirty-three persons in all from Leah.
Judah's sons: Er and Onan died in Canaan; Pharez and Zarah, with Hezron and Hamul. Issachar's and Zebulun's lines. Leah bore these and her daughter Dinah — thirty-three persons in all from Leah.

Judah's sons: Er and Onan died in Canaan; Pharez and Zarah, with Hezron and Hamul. Issachar's and Zebulun's lines. Leah bore these and her daughter Dinah — thirty-three persons in all from Leah.
Gad's sons. Asher's, including his daughter Sarah. These are Zilpah's line — sixteen persons whom Zilpah bore to Jacob.
Gad's sons. Asher's, including his daughter Sarah. These are Zilpah's line — sixteen persons whom Zilpah bore to Jacob.
Gad's sons. Asher's, including his daughter Sarah. These are Zilpah's line — sixteen persons whom Zilpah bore to Jacob.

Rachel's sons: Joseph and Benjamin. Joseph's two sons — Manasseh and Ephraim — were born to him in Egypt by Asenath. Benjamin's ten sons are listed; his line will not be small. Fourteen persons from Rachel.

Rachel's sons: Joseph and Benjamin. Joseph's two sons — Manasseh and Ephraim — were born to him in Egypt by Asenath. Benjamin's ten sons are listed; his line will not be small. Fourteen persons from Rachel.

Rachel's sons: Joseph and Benjamin. Joseph's two sons — Manasseh and Ephraim — were born to him in Egypt by Asenath. Benjamin's ten sons are listed; his line will not be small. Fourteen persons from Rachel.
Dan's son, Naphtali's sons — these are the children of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel. Seven persons in her line. The text counts every name carefully.
Dan's son, Naphtali's sons — these are the children of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel. Seven persons in her line. The text counts every name carefully.
Dan's son, Naphtali's sons — these are the children of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to Rachel. Seven persons in her line. The text counts every name carefully.

All those who came with Jacob into Egypt — his own descendants, not counting his sons' wives — were sixty-six. With Joseph and his two sons already in Egypt, the house of Jacob numbers seventy. The small family Abraham was promised is now seventy strong.

All those who came with Jacob into Egypt — his own descendants, not counting his sons' wives — were sixty-six. With Joseph and his two sons already in Egypt, the house of Jacob numbers seventy. The small family Abraham was promised is now seventy strong.

Jacob sends Judah ahead to Joseph to find the way to Goshen. Joseph makes his chariot ready and goes up to meet his father. He falls on his neck and weeps a good while. Israel says: now let me die, since I have seen your face — you are alive.

Jacob sends Judah ahead to Joseph to find the way to Goshen. Joseph makes his chariot ready and goes up to meet his father. He falls on his neck and weeps a good while. Israel says: now let me die, since I have seen your face — you are alive.

Jacob sends Judah ahead to Joseph to find the way to Goshen. Joseph makes his chariot ready and goes up to meet his father. He falls on his neck and weeps a good while. Israel says: now let me die, since I have seen your face — you are alive.

Joseph plans his brothers' meeting with Pharaoh. I will tell him: my brothers and my father have come from Canaan; they are shepherds. When Pharaoh asks your occupation, tell him you have been cattle-keepers from youth, like our fathers — so you may live in Goshen. Every shepherd is an abomination to the Egyptians. The cultural distance becomes a protection.
